# Lygodactylus

> Open-source AI agent desktop app for Windows & macOS

Lygodactylus is a free, open-source desktop application that provides a local AI agent workspace. It wraps Claude Code, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, and other AI models into a user-friendly GUI with one-click installation, VM-level sandbox isolation, and built-in productivity tools. Lygodactylus is the open-source implementation of Claude Cowork, designed to make AI-powered desktop automation accessible to everyone.

## Key Features

- One-click install for Windows and macOS — no terminal or coding required
- VM-level sandbox isolation (WSL2 on Windows, Lima on macOS)
- Multi-model support: Claude, OpenAI GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek, GLM, MiniMax, Kimi, and any OpenAI-compatible API
- Built-in Skills system for generating PPTX, DOCX, XLSX, PDF documents
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool integration — browser, Notion, desktop apps
- GUI automation via computer use (recommended model: Gemini-3-Pro)
- Remote control through Feishu (Lark) and Slack
- Real-time trace panel for AI reasoning and tool execution
- Custom skill creation and management via skill-creator toolkit

## Links

- Source repository: https://github.com/Emilien-Etadam/lygodactylus
- Releases: https://github.com/Emilien-Etadam/lygodactylus/releases
- Issues: https://github.com/Emilien-Etadam/lygodactylus/issues
- Discord: https://discord.gg/pynjtQDf
- License: MIT

## Installation

### macOS (Homebrew)
```
brew tap OpenCoworkAI/tap
brew install --cask --no-quarantine lygodactylus
```

### Windows / macOS (Installer)
Download from: https://github.com/Emilien-Etadam/lygodactylus/releases

## Supported AI Providers

| Provider       | Base URL                                 | Recommended Model     |
|----------------|------------------------------------------|-----------------------|
| OpenRouter     | https://openrouter.ai/api                | claude-4-5-sonnet     |
| Anthropic      | (Default)                                | claude-4-5-sonnet     |
| Zhipu AI (GLM) | https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/anthropic   | glm-4.7, glm-4.6     |
| MiniMax        | https://api.minimaxi.com/anthropic       | minimax-m2            |
| Kimi           | https://api.kimi.com/coding/             | kimi-k2               |

## FAQ

### What is Lygodactylus?
Lygodactylus is a free, open-source desktop application that provides a local AI agent workspace. It wraps AI models (Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek, etc.) into a GUI with one-click installers for Windows and macOS — no terminal or coding knowledge required.

### How is Lygodactylus different from Claude Cowork?
Lygodactylus is the open-source implementation of Claude Cowork. It adds multi-model support (not just Claude), GUI automation via computer use, remote control through Feishu/Slack, and VM-level sandbox isolation.

### What AI models does Lygodactylus support?
Claude (via Anthropic or OpenRouter), OpenAI-compatible APIs, and Chinese models including GLM (Zhipu AI), MiniMax, and Kimi. Any provider offering an OpenAI-compatible API endpoint can be configured.

### Is Lygodactylus free?
Yes. Lygodactylus itself is completely free and open-source under the MIT license. You only need to pay for the AI model API usage from your chosen provider.

### Does Lygodactylus work on Linux?
Currently, Lygodactylus provides pre-built installers for Windows and macOS only. Linux users can build from source.

### How does sandbox isolation work?
Lygodactylus offers multi-level protection: basic path-based restrictions on all platforms, and enhanced VM-level isolation using WSL2 (Windows) or Lima (macOS). When a VM is available, all commands execute inside an isolated Linux environment.

### What are Skills?
Skills are built-in workflows for specific tasks like generating PPTX, DOCX, PDF, or XLSX files. Lygodactylus ships with default skills and includes a skill-creator tool to help you build custom ones.

### What is MCP?
MCP (Model Context Protocol) lets AI connect to external tools and services. Lygodactylus supports MCP connectors for browsers, Notion, and other desktop apps.

### Is my data safe?
Lygodactylus runs locally on your machine. Your files stay in your workspace. The only external communication is with the AI model API you configure. No data is sent to Lygodactylus servers.
